title: Teleporter Control
description: A teleporter control, basic and fun to use!
body:
![TC ICON](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/181774887af0e0400ac8b1ebdf6722c6b75ec649.png)
 

 
## **A teleporter control, basic and fun to use!
## 
##  
## 
# How to use?**

 

By holding the SHIFT key and clicking on a block, its current position will be PRINTED or MARKED
If you have the Battery item in your inventory, you can use it, if you don't have a Battery in your inventory it will not work
With a marked location, by clicking in the air with the right mouse button, you will teleport to the marked position, leaving you on cooldown
 

Not only is there a control that will teleport you, there are several new items and blocks for you to explore, if you have any doubts download and install the Just Enough Items MOD, this mod will give you the possibility to see the recipes from my mod

 

# What is Teleportation?

 

The user is able to make their body simply disappear from the place where it is and reappear in a new desired location, performing a type of "instant movement". Generally, he can make this ability affect any type of matter and energy, being able to teleport not only himself, but also other individuals, objects and, in some cases, non-physical bodies. In combat, the user can combine their teleportation with different forms of attack and defense, creating a highly fast, precise and strategic fighting style.

There are several ways to make teleportation happen. Most users rely on making the atoms of a body travel from one place to another at a speed great enough for the act to be instantaneous. However, there are several other ways to produce the same effect: distorting the surrounding space-time to travel between dimensional gaps and "shorten the path", getting involved with elements and moving along with them, etc.

Note: Feats of speed and teleportation are different, those who teleport are not using speed, as while speed is a division of space and time, teleportation is instantaneous (not using time) and goes directly to the exit point (not taking up space in the path).
